lines: The guard lies on his back in blood that has not finished drying. Whoever killed him kept stabbing after death, yet left the chapel key tied inside his sleeve.
His coat is folded open with deliberate care. Five cuts meet in a point-down star over the sternum. Beneath it, carved deeper: NOT HOLY. DO NOT FEAST.

Wound-stars are common enough on cult roads, but this verdict is not. The killers tested him and refused what they found. They kept the two guards they believed were changing, one on the cross and one below, while this faithful man was left as a warning and not a meal. No cult in your Censure records made that distinction.
You close the coat over their judgment. It cannot restore his dignity, but leaving the words exposed would grant the killers one last use of him.
